offer.what: Employee benefits consulting and brokerage for small businesses — voluntary benefits including short & long-term disability, cancer, hospital indemnity, group health insurance, dental, and vision coverage
offer.coreOutcome: Small businesses get competitive, affordable benefits packages that help them attract and retain talent on par with larger employers
offer.pricePoint: unknown — likely carrier-commission model (no direct fee to employer) with possible consulting arrangements; no pricing published
offer.commitment: Annual benefits plan enrollment/renewal cycle; ongoing consulting relationship

audience.icp: Small business owners and HR decision-makers across industries in Louisiana (primarily Baton Rouge area) who are losing talent or struggling to compete on benefits
painPoints: Cannot afford or navigate comprehensive benefits packages that large employers offer
Losing employees or failing to attract talent due to weak benefits
Overwhelmed by the complexity of insurance options and compliance
Feel locked out of voluntary and group benefit products due to company size
segments: Small business owners (1–50 employees) with no current benefits
Small businesses with basic benefits looking to add voluntary/supplemental coverage
HR managers at growing SMBs needing a benefits refresh

salesCycle.length: weeks to months — B2B relationship sale requiring discovery, proposal, and enrollment
salesCycle.trial: unknown — likely a free consultation/needs assessment
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Direct outreach to John Varnado via contact form or phone; proposal and enrollment managed personally
salesCycle.topObjection: Cost concerns — small business owners assume comprehensive benefits are too expensive or only accessible to large companies

ahaMoment: First enrollment completed — employees actively using their new benefits and the owner seeing tangible recruitment/retention lift

retention.recurring: Yes — annual open enrollment and plan renewal cycle creates a natural recurring touchpoint
retention.expansion: Adding voluntary benefit lines (e.g., adding dental/vision on top of health, or adding disability coverage), increasing covered employee headcount as business grows
retention.churnRisk: Client switches carriers or brokers at annual renewal; relationship-driven so risk is low if value/communication is maintained

lifecycleGaps: No visible lead magnet or content offer to capture top-of-funnel leads
No welcome or onboarding sequence for new clients
No nurture sequence for prospects who inquire but don't convert immediately
No reminder/check-in sequence around annual enrollment windows
No referral or advocacy program visible
No re-engagement sequence for cold leads
No value newsletter or educational content program
toneVoice: Professional but approachable — positions as a trusted advisor helping the 'little guy' compete; warm, knowledgeable, not corporate-cold; conversational with authority
compliance: Insurance/benefits brokerage — must comply with state insurance licensing disclosures (Louisiana DOI), CAN-SPAM, and any applicable ACA/ERISA disclosures in communications. Not HIPAA-covered entity directly but handles benefit plan data — exercise care with health-related messaging.
